Choose an ODE Solver
Ordinary Differential Equations
An ordinary differential equation (ODE) contains one or more derivatives of a dependent variable, y, with respect to a single independent variable, t. The order of the ODE is equal to the highest-order derivative.

Basic Solver Selection
- ode45: Most of the time. First choice for nonstiff problems.
- ode23: For crude tolerances or moderate stiffness.
- ode113: For stringent error tolerances or expensive ODE functions.
- ode15s: Use when ode45 fails or is inefficient (suspected stiffness) or for DAEs.
- ode23s: For crude tolerances on stiff problems.
- ode15i: For fully implicit problems and index-1 DAEs.

Summary Table (Abridged):
| Solver | Problem Type | Accuracy | When to Use |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| ode45 | Nonstiff | Medium | First choice. |
| ode15s | Stiff | Low-Medium | If ode45 fails. |
